title: BFT Council as Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Router
filing_authority: MEOK Sovereign Substrate (SOV3) — IP-First AI Operating System
abstract: A Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) council of 33 nodes that simultaneously functions as a learned router for a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language model. Each expert is gated by quorum-signed ballot weight, producing an audit-trail novelty over standard learned routers: the routing decision itself is BFT-attested and cryptographically provable. The combination of consensus gating + expert dispatch + on-chain attestation produces emergent properties unavailable to either BFT-only or MoE-only systems: adversarial experts can be slashed by quorum, while honest experts receive compounding routing share proportional to verified correctness across 33 nodes.

claims.claim: A method comprising: (a) receiving a user query at a BFT council of N>=33 nodes; (b) each node computing an independent expert-affinity score against K available experts; (c) nodes broadcasting affinity vectors via signed ballots; (d) quorum (>=22/33) reaching consensus on the routing weight vector w ∈ R^K; (e) dispatching the query to the top-k experts weighted by w; (f) returning the aggregated response with a quorum-attested audit trail.

The method of claim 1, wherein the affinity score is computed as a function of (i) expert load, (ii) historical reward (correctness votes), and (iii) query-expert cosine similarity in a learned embedding space.

The method of claim 1, further comprising a slashing protocol: experts whose outputs receive <50% quorum approval across M>=10 consecutive dispatches are removed from the active set and a soulbound SBT is burned.

novelty_distinction: Standard MoE routers (Shazeer 2017, Fedus 2022) use a learned gate network trained end-to-end. No prior art uses consensus as the gating mechanism.
Standard BFT systems (Castro-Liskov 1999, Tendermint 2014) reach consensus on a value, not on a routing decision. The MoE-BFT composition is novel.
The audit-trail novelty: every routing decision is provably the result of a quorum, not a single point of failure.
implementation_status: LIVE in SOV3 sovereign-temple. 33-node BFT council with K=11 brains (KIMI K2.7-CODE, KIMI K2.7-THINKING, Claude Opus 4.8, Hermes 1, OpenClaw, Qwen3 0.6b/8b, Gemma3 4b, Moondream, DeepSeek V4 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Pro). 5ms consensus.
